title: Add photo deletion and local saved-photo gallery to Photo Scanner

requirements.summary: Add an accessible delete/trash control to clear the current Photo Scanner session and reset editor + camera state.
acceptanceCriteria: When a photo is loaded in the Photo Scanner, a delete/trash icon is visible and accessible (keyboard + screen reader label).
Activating delete clears the current image, stops/close camera preview if applicable, resets zoom/rotation/enhance state, and returns the user to the capture/upload choices.
Delete action does not break the existing Download/New Photo flows.

file_operations.description: Add a dedicated trash/delete icon button (with aria-label) when a photo is loaded; on activation, clear selected image, reset image editor state, clear file input value, and ensure any active camera preview is stopped/closed via the existing camera hook (Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.)

requirements.summary: Implement a local saved-photo gallery/backup for Photo Scanner with save, view/re-open, and delete actions persisted in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can save the current scan into a local gallery from the Photo Scanner UI (user-facing text in English).
Saved photos persist across page refreshes in the same browser (local backup).
The gallery displays saved photos as a thumbnail grid/list with timestamp (or human-readable date) and provides actions to: open/view a saved scan and delete a saved scan.
Deleting a saved scan removes it from the gallery immediately and it does not return after refresh.
If there are no saved photos, the gallery shows an empty state message in English.
The gallery works on mobile and desktop layouts (responsive).

file_operations.description: Create a hook to manage a locally persisted saved-scan gallery using browser storage (e.g., localStorage): load on startup, add/save current scan (store image data URL plus created timestamp), list items, delete items, and provide a function to open/re-hydrate a saved scan into the Photo Scanner.

Create a responsive SavedScansGallery component that renders saved scans as a thumbnail grid/list with a human-readable date, and provides actions to open/re-open a scan and request deletion; show an English empty state when no items are present.

Wire the local gallery into the Photo Scanner UI: add an English 'Save to Gallery' action when a scan is present (saving the processed image if available), render the SavedScansGallery within the scanner view, and support opening a saved scan back into the editor without breaking existing Download/New Photo flows.

requirements.summary: Add an accessible confirmation step before deleting saved scans from the local gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Attempting to delete a saved photo prompts the user with a confirmation dialog or inline confirmation UI in English.
Confirm removes the saved photo; cancel leaves it unchanged.
The confirmation UI is accessible (focus trapped if modal; buttons reachable via keyboard).

file_operations.description: Add an English confirmation step for destructive deletion (inline confirm/cancel UI per item or a modal if already available in the project), ensuring keyboard accessibility and clear focus behavior; only delete the item after explicit confirmation.
Ensure deletion updates in-memory state and browser storage immediately so removed items do not return after refresh; expose deletion helpers needed by the confirmation flow.
